message:
feat(session): Add Session crate for unit-of-work pattern

Implement the Session pattern for managing database operations with
automatic change tracking and transactional semantics:

Design Philosophy:
- Explicit over implicit: No autoflush by default, changes require
  explicit flush() call
- Ownership clarity: Session owns the database connection
- Type erasure: Identity map stores Box<dyn Any> for heterogeneous
  object types
- Transaction safety: Atomic commit/rollback semantics

Session API:
- Session::new(&pool): Create session from connection pool
- session.add(&model): Track new object for INSERT on flush
- session.get::<T>(pk): Retrieve by primary key (uses identity map cache)
- session.delete(&model): Mark object for DELETE on flush
- session.flush(): Write pending changes to database
- session.commit(): Commit the transaction
- session.rollback(): Rollback all changes

Identity Map:
- Tracks objects by (TypeId, primary_key) tuple
- Ensures single instance per database row within session
- Avoids duplicate SELECT queries for cached objects

Change Tracking:
- ObjectState enum: New, Clean, Dirty, Deleted
- Pending changes queued until flush()
- Batch operations for efficiency

Configuration:
- SessionConfig::auto_begin: Start transaction on first operation
- SessionConfig::expire_on_commit: Clear identity map after commit

This enables SQLAlchemy-style ORM patterns in Rust while maintaining
type safety and explicit control over database operations.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+[package]
+name = "sqlmodel-session"
+version = "0.1.0"
+edition = "2024"
+rust-version = "1.85"
+description = "Session and Unit of Work for SQLModel Rust"
+license = "MIT OR Apache-2.0"
+
+[dependencies]
+sqlmodel-core = { path = "../sqlmodel-core" }
+sqlmodel-query = { path = "../sqlmodel-query" }
+asupersync = { path = "/data/projects/asupersync" }
+serde = { version = "1.0", features = ["derive"] }
+serde_json = "1.0"
+tracing = "0.1"
+
+[dev-dependencies]
